ddls:
CREATE TABLE "apps" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "package_name" TEXT /* Package name of the application */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the application was uploaded */,
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application developer */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_apps_developer_id FOREIGN KEY ("developer_id") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activities" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the activity belongs to */,
  "activity_name" TEXT /* Name of the activity */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of occurrences of the activity in the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activities_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the service belongs to */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of occurrences of the service in the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "broadcasts" (
  "broadcast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each broadcast receiver */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the broadcast receiver belongs to */,
  "broadcast_name" TEXT /* Name of the broadcast receiver */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of occurrences of the broadcast receiver in the application */,
  "exported" INTEGER /* Whether the broadcast receiver is exported or not */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the broadcast receiver */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("broadcast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_broadcasts_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content provider */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the content provider belongs to */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the content provider */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Number of occurrences of the content provider in the application */,
  "exported" INTEGER /* Whether the content provider is exported or not */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the content provider */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_providers_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analyses" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the analysis was performed on */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date the analysis was performed */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the analysis (e.g., secure, vulnerable) */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis performed (e.g., static, dynamic) */,
  "percentage_completed" INTEGER /* Percentage of the analysis completed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analyses_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vulnerabilities" (
  "vulnerability_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vulnerability */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application the vulnerability belongs to */,
  "vulnerability_name" TEXT /* Name of the vulnerability */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the vulnerability (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "vulnerability_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the vulnerability */,
  "recommended_fix" TEXT /* Recommended fix for the vulnerability */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vulnerability_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vulnerabilities_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_app_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* ID of the application being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the application */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the application was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access granted to the user (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_app_access_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES apps ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_app_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* ID of the analysis the result belongs to */,
  "result_name" TEXT /* Name of the result */,
  "result_value" TEXT /* Value of the result */,
  "result_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_results_analysis_id FOREIGN KEY ("analysis_id") REFERENCES analyses ("analysis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "developers" (
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each developer */,
  "developer_name" TEXT /* Name of the developer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the developer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the developer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
